definition: New-issue concessions in high-yield markets have widened to 15-25bp in 2025-2026 from near-zero during QE, representing a specific and observable QT transmission channel reflecting reduced dealer balance sheet capacity and greater price sensitivity among the marginal buyer base.
evidence: HY new-issue concessions at 15-25bp (2025-2026) vs near-zero during QE
open_questions: Will new-issue concessions continue to widen as QT proceeds?
Are current concession levels adequate to compensate for reduced dealer capacity?
